abt.com.lead: dashAI is an academic project of the Department of Computer Science (DCC) at the Faculty of Physical and Mathematical Sciences (FCFM), University of Chile — host institution and principal collaborator. It is built in collaboration with CENIA, Unholster and other allies, with funding from ANID. Its development brings together academic maintainers, undergraduate and graduate students, external testers, and anyone who opens an issue, translates the UI or publishes a plugin to PyPI. Every PR counts. Every Discord thread leaves a mark.
abt.g.p: Technical decisions are discussed on GitHub Issues and Discord. Institutional roadmap decisions go through the maintainers committee. Release priorities respond to external tester feedback (the latest round with Unholster defined the pre-release sprint).
abt.hist.alum.n: // PLACEHOLDER · list student contributors with (1) name, (2) affiliation at the time of contribution, (3) year(s), (4) area of contribution. Collect consent before publishing. Meanwhile, the commit history tells the story: contributors graph.
abt.hist.alum.p: Thesis students from undergrad and grad programs, research assistants. Many current abstractions started as course projects or theses.
abt.hist.cta.p: If you worked on dashAI at any point — as a student, thesis writer, volunteer, partner institution — write to us. We want to acknowledge you explicitly on this page.

abt.hist.inst.usm.r: research & development in early phases · no longer an active collaborator
abt.hist.lead: dashAI has been under construction for several years. Undergrad and grad students passed through the project leaving commits, thesis students contributed specific components, and institutions collaborated in early phases that no longer participate today. This section recognizes that accumulated work.
abt.inst.anid.n: National Agency for Research and Development
abt.inst.lead: Five Chilean public institutions keep the project active in 2026. Each card has reserved space for the official logo — if your institution wants to provide it as SVG or PNG, we integrate it directly.
abt.lead: Chilean academic project developed and maintained by a network of five institutions, funded with public funds. Sustainability comes from public funding and the community that builds it day after day.
abt.m.p: Reduce technical barriers to the use of Machine Learning in research, teaching and applied work, while preserving the extensibility required by specialized groups. dashAI provides a visual interface over a modular architecture, auditable and open-source.
abt.v.p2: Consolidate a Chilean visual Machine Learning platform with three central attributes: local operation on user data, extensibility via community plugins, and academic traceability of its components. The project aspires to serve as shared infrastructure between research groups, public institutions and professionals.
